| GLM-5.1 | 50.4 | 47.5 | -2.9 | 42.5 | 38.9 | -3.7 | [...] # EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments

EvoArena evaluates LLM agents in environments where terminal workflows, software repositories, and user preferences evolve over time. EvoMem augments agent memory with patch histories that preserve what changed, why it changed, and when old behavior still matters.

Jundong Xu1,\ Qingchuan Li1,\ Jiaying Wu1 Yihuai Lan2 Shuyue Stella Li3 Huichi Zhou4 Bowen Jiang5 Lei Wang3 Jun Wang4 Anh Tuan Luu6 Caiming Xiong7 Hae Won Park8 Bryan Hooi1 Zhiyuan Hu1,8

Other titles which will see time on the showcase stage include the newly released Invincible VS (which will also reward its champion the Arm of Conquest), Vampire Savior, Blazblue: Central Fiction, Under Night In-Birth II Sys:Celes and Virtua Fighter 5 REVO World Stage.

For those looking for the full breakdown of this year’s EVO Las Vegas entrants, you can find the data on the tournament’s website via this blog post. While the call for entrants has closed, you can still attend the event as a spectator, registration can be found here. [...] Gaming Age

results.title: EvoArena: Memory Paradigms for Evolving Environments
results.content:
### Description
29 views
Posted: 12 Jun 2026 [...] EvoArena is a new benchmark platform designed to assess how well large-scale language model (LLM) agents adapt to changing dynamic environments over time. The system measures performance in three key areas that are constantly evolving, such as non-remnuous terminal work, software development, and changes in user preferences. The researchers found that existing agents are vulnerable to environmental changes, and proposed an innovative memory storage method called EvoMem, which records memory update history in the form of patches. EvoMem helps agents be flexible in new situations and improve their ability to perform chain tasks by preserving valid information from the past and the basis for change. As a result of the experiment, the agents who introduced this method showed improved accuracy [...] agents who introduced this method showed improved accuracy across the indicators, demonstrating high reliability and adaptability in the actual distribution environment. As a result, these sources emphasize that future AI agents should understand, remember, and act more accurately in the changing world.

 agents have achieved strong performance on a wide

range of benchmarks, yet most evaluations assume static environments. In contrast,

real-world deployment is inherently dynamic, requiring agents to continually align

their knowledge, skills, and behavior with changing environments and updated task

conditions.To address this gap, we introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that

models environment changes as sequences of progressive updates across terminal,

software, and social domains. We further propose EvoMem, a patch-based memory

paradigm that records memory evolution as structured update histories, enabling agents [...] HorizonBench Personalization Preference changes△✓✗

EvoArena Multi-domain Dynamic Env✓✓✓

Table 1:Comparison of EvoArena with related agent benchmarks.PE denotes persistent environment

evolution, where the same environment or user state evolves across versions; IC denotes implicit change, where

the agent must infer or handle changes from context or interaction; CE denotes chain evaluation, where success

is measured over temporally linked task sequences.✓denotes supported,✗denotes not supported, and△denotes

partial support.

• We introduce EvoArena, a benchmark suite that evaluates agents across progressively

evolving workflow, software, and social environments.

Keep in mind, Rivals II had already qualified for the Arena Stage long before this, and going from #5 to #4 actually makes no difference in the prizepool payout.

PS: This isn't the first time he decided to cover other people's tournament registration fees out of his own pocket in order to make it _the biggest tournament ever_. Melee registration at Supernova 2025 was free thanks to Ludwig's push to make it the biggest Melee tournament ever, which everyone were grateful for.

I wonder how many hentai games will show up on the top 50 demos list…

## Mintrocket studio head Jaeho Hwang shares new details regarding Bancho the Chef during their Dev Dive video

Only the power of PlayStation can fully showcase the backstory of the proprietor of Bancho… [...] Street Fighter, even without having Tifa Lockhart on the roster has managed to maintain it’s #1 spot that it’s held for years with 2414 competitors. Tekken locks in the 2nd place slot with 1354 players in a year which saw the departure of Katsuhiro Harada and other key members of the development team. Despite dev layoffs, interest in Riot’s free to play fighter 2XKO remained strong and the title managed to nab the third slot.

What could be attributed to a serious push by publisher Lugwig Ahgren (via Offbrand Games), Rivals of Aether 2 will have its largest tournament and will crown their champion at the end of Saturday night. Joining the platform fighter are two titles who are no strangers to appearing in the arena with Guilty Gear -STRIVE- and Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising. [...] # Street Fighter is set to close out EVO Las Vegas once again as the tournament’s main stage schedule is revealed

We are a mere two weeks away from what could be described as the Super Bowl of the fighting game community. EVO Las Vegas emanating from the Las Vegas Convention Center from June 26-28th will crown champions across 12 titles. While every game should be seen in the spectacle of the EVO Arena, time permitting only 6 will get the opportunity and these six are chosen based on competitor registration counts. Today EVO finally revealed these numbers and we have our line up.

Photo by ItsMeHere on June 15, 2026. May be a graphic of text that says '01 LLM AGENTS FAIL WHEN REALITY SHIFTS EvoArena exposes the deployment gap nobody talks about'.

itsmehere.in's profile picture

itsmehere.in

•

Follow

itsmehere.in's profile picture

itsmehere.in [...] LLM agents are crushing benchmarks — then dying in the wild.  
  
EvoArena is the first benchmark that tests what actually breaks in deployment: environments that evolve. Most evals freeze the world in time. Your prod environment doesn't.  
  
The gap? Agents trained on static data can't unlearn outdated patterns when rules change, tasks mutate, or context rewrites itself. Memory systems that worked yesterday become liabilities tomorrow.  
  
If you're deploying agents, this benchmark exposes the blind spot between eval scores and real reliability. Test against evolution, not snapshots.  
  
Which failure mode have you hit hardest in prod?
